key_departures.impact: Starting center signed a 3-year, $25M deal with Detroit. Mays had emerged as a dependable starter in 2025 and was ranked 24th among qualifying centers by PFF. His departure leaves a glaring, immediate hole at center with no proven replacement on the roster.

Dowdle posted back-to-back 1,000-yard rushing seasons (1,076 yards, 6 TD in 2025 on 236 carries) and was a key piece in Carolina's NFC South title run. His departure to Pittsburgh on a 2-year, $12.25M deal creates a need at starting RB. Chuba Hubbard (whose YPC dropped from 4.8 to 3.8 in 2025) and an oft-injured Jonathon Brooks are the remaining options.

Ekwonu ruptured his patellar tendon in the Wild Card loss to the Rams on Jan. 10. Recovery is 6-12 months, meaning he is all but certain to miss the 2026 season. He counts $17.56M fully guaranteed against the cap on his 5th-year option. This is a pre-existing but now critical OT need — effectively an in-season injury departure.

Dalton's 3-year tenure as Bryce Young's backup/mentor is ending. Expected to be released after the Pickett signing. Was 0-1 as a starter in 2025 and 1-6 over the past 3 seasons. Depth loss, but intentional — team wanted a younger backup.

Starting LB hitting open market. Rozeboom was serviceable but not a needle-mover — PFF noted the 'move to bring in Rozeboom last offseason didn't pan out.' His departure reinforces the pre-existing LB need which Morgan had already targeted.
Rotational edge rusher hitting free agency. Loss is moderate depth — Panthers were already thin at EDGE before the Phillips signing.
Starting safety entering free market. Panthers still have Tre'Von Moehrig on a long-term deal, but Scott's departure creates a potential hole opposite him.

signings.analysis: Top-of-market deal — $30M AAV is elite tier for EDGE ($25M+ = elite). Phillips ranked 9th in the NFL in QB pressures (73) in 2025 across stints with Miami and Philadelphia. He is 26, ascending, and fits DC Ejiro Evero's 3-4 scheme. The $80M in guarantees signal full commitment. EDGE was Carolina's most critical need (tied 28th in sacks with 30 in 2025). This signing, combined with the return of Patrick Jones II (when healthy), fully addresses the pass rush need. Need drops from tier 1 critical to tier 4/depth.

Lloyd is a 2022 first-round pick (27th overall) who earned his first Pro Bowl and second-team All-Pro honors in 2025, generating 5 interceptions after recording just 4 total in his first 3 seasons. His $15M AAV ties him for 5th-highest-paid LB in the league. $25M fully guaranteed is a meaningful commitment. GM Morgan had explicitly identified LB as the priority to 'attack' this offseason. Lloyd will take the green-dot defensive signal-caller role. 3-year deal with real guarantees means this is solved for the foreseeable future. LB drops from tier 1 critical to tier 3/4.

One-year deal replaces Andy Dalton as Bryce Young's backup. Pickett (2022 first-round pick, 27 career starts) provides more upside than Dalton as a developmental option. However, this is a one-year bridge deal with $4M guaranteed — explicitly a backup role. Bryce Young remains the unquestioned starter. QB depth is addressed but QB1 succession is still a future need if Young doesn't progress.

One-year re-signing keeps a depth/special teams CB in place. Evans played 17 games in 2025, logging 37.7% of special teams snaps and occasionally filling in as CB2 in Horn's absence. Deal terms not officially disclosed but described as likely insignificant. This does not address starter-level CB depth; Jaycee Horn remains the CB1, but the CB2 question lingers. Minimal needle-mover.
Jansen re-signed for a record 18th season with the Panthers. Pure special teams — no draft need implications.

Exclusive Rights Free Agent tender — Coker had no choice but to accept. He was a solid complementary WR to Tetairoa McMillan down the stretch (19 catches, 244 yards, 3 TDs in final 5 games; 9/134/1 in Wild Card). Minimum-level tender. Retained for depth/slot. No draft need impact.
ERFA tender, pure camp/depth retention. No material need impact.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Phillips signing (4yr/$120M, $80M gtd, $30M AAV) is elite-tier and fully resolves the pass rush need. Phillips ranked 9th in pressures (73) in 2025. With Patrick Jones II potentially returning healthy, EDGE is no longer a premium need. At most, a developmental Day 3 flier.

Devin Lloyd signing (3yr/$45M, $25M gtd) brings a Pro Bowl, All-Pro second-team LB to replace the underwhelming Rozeboom/Wallace tandem. Lloyd is 27 and a long-term solution. LB drops significantly — may still want depth via mid-rounds but starter need is solved.

Cade Mays (starting C) departed to Detroit on a 3yr/$25M deal. No center has been signed. The Panthers have zero proven starting center options on the roster. This is now the most pressing unaddressed offensive need heading into the draft. Must target in rounds 1-3 or sign a veteran free agent.

Ikem Ekwonu's patellar tendon rupture (Jan. 10) effectively removes him from the 2026 season. He counts $17.56M against the cap but won't play. Yosh Nijman (also a free agent) has not been re-signed as of this update. No OT solution has been confirmed in free agency. This is tied with IOL as the most urgent offensive need — must address via FA or the draft at pick 19.

Rico Dowdle (1,076 rushing yards, 6 TD in 2025) signed with Pittsburgh. Remaining options are Chuba Hubbard (declining efficiency, 3.8 YPC) and Jonathon Brooks (missed all of 2025 with second ACL tear). The lead back role is unsettled. Mid-round draft pick or veteran FA addition warranted.
Nick Scott is a UFA and has not been re-signed. Tre'Von Moehrig anchors one spot, but no confirmed CB2/S2 solution has been added. Safety depth is a legitimate need heading into the draft.
Akayleb Evans re-signed on a one-year depth deal. Jaycee Horn (CB1) remains. But CB2 behind Horn is still depth-level only. Need is unchanged — still worth targeting in mid rounds of the draft.

Kenny Pickett (1yr/$7.5M) provides credible backup depth behind Bryce Young. One-year deal is a bridge, not a long-term answer — but the immediate depth need is satisfied. QB is no longer a pressing draft need in 2026.

key_additions.headline: Elite pass rusher (9th in NFL pressures in 2025) fills Panthers' most critical defensive need on a top-of-market deal
Pro Bowl, All-Pro LB directly addresses GM Morgan's top stated priority; brings coverage excellence (5 INTs in 2025) and green-dot leadership
Former first-round pick upgrades backup QB room, pushing Andy Dalton out as Bryce Young's No. 2
Special teams stalwart and CB depth retained on affordable one-year deal

key_losses.headline: Starting center departs to Detroit, leaving Panthers with an immediate and urgent void at the position
Back-to-back 1,000-yard rusher and key piece of NFC South title run signs with Pittsburgh, leaving Panthers' backfield unsettled
Starting LT lost for likely all of 2026 after patellar tendon rupture in Wild Card; $17.56M cap hit stays while team must find a replacement
Starting LB departs as UFA, but need was already addressed with Devin Lloyd signing
Three-year backup and Bryce Young mentor expected to be released as Panthers pivot to younger backup option in Pickett

notes: All signings reported by Ian Rapoport, Mike Garafolo, Adam Schefter, and Tom Pelissero during the March 9 legal tampering window. Contracts become official March 11 at 4 p.m. ET. Joshua Jobe (CB) listed on Panthers' FA list with 'Re-signing with team' per NFL.com tracker — terms not reported as of this update; treated as low-value depth retention. Cap space figures (~$9M pre-FA per OverTheCap) were already extremely tight before Phillips and Lloyd signings; both deals are likely backloaded with significant restructuring required. Post-FA cap is estimated near zero or negative pending restructures. The Ekwonu OT situation is treated as an in-season injury departure per methodology. No OT FA signing for Carolina confirmed as of 2026-03-10.
